text.title: Product Development Trends 2026: Bucket Rubric and Snapshot Workflow
text.summary: Four trend buckets with the state/confidence scoring rubric, snapshot JSON schema, and quarterly diff-to-ticket workflow for keeping the trend list grounded in dated primary sources.

list.item: AI-augmented ideation: pattern recognition at scale, gap identification, surfacing weak signals from user research
Continuous customer feedback: real-time product iteration, weekly interview cadence, assumption testing loops
Rapid pivots: quarterly vs annual adjustment cycles, agile delivery over waterfall planning
Cross-functional teams: PM + Design + Engineering collaboration as the delivery unit

rule.statement: Treat the four buckets as a rubric to score against dated primary sources — not as settled facts. Each quarter, re-derive the state of each bucket rather than quoting the previous snapshot.
rule.rationale: The buckets are 2024-vintage consultancy talking points. Without re-verification they age into outdated claims that miscalibrate roadmap and GTM decisions.

rule.statement: Score each bucket on: state ∈ {accepted, contested, declining, emerging}, confidence ∈ [0,1] (require ≥2 cited primary sources for confidence ≥0.6), and 3 supporting URLs with publication date ≤12 months.
rule.rationale: A four-state rubric with confidence and source requirements makes trend claims falsifiable. "State = accepted, confidence = 0.4, one source" communicates very different certainty than "accepted, confidence = 0.8, three sources".

rule.statement: Pin primary sources to: Stack Overflow Annual Survey, GitHub Octoverse, ThoughtWorks Tech Radar, State of DevOps (DORA), Gartner Hype Cycle. Reject any source older than the previous quarterly snapshot.
rule.rationale: These are the authoritative annual/quarterly measurements of actual developer and team practices — not analyst opinion pieces or vendor blog posts.

rule.statement: Write snapshots to .research/snapshots/trends-YYYY-QQ.md and a JSON sidecar, never edit README.md to "update trends". The README is the rubric, not the answer.
rule.rationale: Editing the rubric with each quarter's findings conflates the measurement instrument with the measurement. Future agents need the original rubric to re-score independently.

rule.statement: When a bucket changes state (e.g., "rapid pivots" moves from accepted to contested), open an SDD ticket in .aidocs/backlog/ with: what changed, which roadmap items are affected, proposed action ≤3 bullets. Never edit roadmap.md directly from the trend scan.
rule.rationale: Automated roadmap edits from trend scans bypass the human approval gate. Routing through SDD backlog ensures a human reviews the implication before it affects delivery commitments.

text.reference: Snapshot JSON schema: 4 buckets with state, confidence, sources, delta_vs_last
Pre-commit validator: 4 buckets, valid state, confidence range, ≥2 sources, no stale sources
